texto: I don't understand computers. I've been unable to construct a working mental model of how they do what they do. I can break software by looking at it. I can blow anything up. Without trying. It's sort of like being a dowser. And this extreme elaborate clumsiness on my part is actually something people will pay me for. It's quite wonderful.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Brenda Laurel: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't understand computers. I've been unable to construct a working mental model of how they do…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que saude-mental, me, people pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e people.
